errors.prefix_vanished_after_post: System with prefix {prefix!r} vanished after POST
errors.no_labeled_functions: {name} (address={address}) has no labeled functions in JMRI's roster — specify a function number, e.g. `throttle on {address} 0`
errors.no_light_labeled_functions: {name} has no light-labeled functions in JMRI's roster (labels are checked for keywords like light/lamp/headlight/lumière/feu/phare) — nothing to switch
errors.exhibition_address_not_allowed: Exhibition mode: address {address} is not in the allowed list ({allowed})
errors.exhibition_power_restricted: Exhibition mode: power cannot be turned on while active — exit exhibition mode first
errors.exhibition_reverse_restricted: Exhibition mode: reverse direction is disabled — forward only while active
errors.exhibition_wrong_password: Wrong password — exhibition mode is still active

group.cache: Manage jmri-cli's own local files (throttle state, shell history)
group.power: Control power to your DCC systems
group.session-start: Power on and wake up every locomotive you've been driving
group.session-end: Stop, park and power off every locomotive you've been driving
group.status: Quick health check: is JMRI reachable, and what's the power state
group.throttle: Drive your locomotives: speed, direction, functions, lights

arg.loco_ref: Locomotive name, a fragment of it, or a DCC address
arg.system_ref_or_default: System name/prefix/fragment (omit for the default system)
arg.function_labels: List a locomotive's user-labeled decoder functions
arg.function_ref_or_every: Function number (0-28) or a fragment of its roster label (omit for every labeled function)
arg.sensor_ref: Sensor system name, userName, or fragment
arg.signal_ref: Signal mast system name, userName, or fragment
arg.block_ref: Block system name, userName, or fragment
arg.ramp_up: Ramp up to the target speed over this many seconds, instead of jumping instantly
arg.ramp_down: Ramp down to the target speed over this many seconds, instead of jumping instantly
arg.hold_seconds: Hold the resulting nonzero speed this long, then auto-stop (mandatory outside the shell whenever the target speed is nonzero)

cache.info: Show the path and on-disk status of each local cache file
cache.clean: Delete jmri-cli's local cache files (throttle state and/or shell history)
cache.clean_state_only: Only clear the cached throttle state (last-known speed/direction/functions per address), leave shell history alone
cache.clean_history_only: Only clear the shell command history, leave the cached throttle state alone

power.status: Show power state of every system
power.on: Turn a system on, or every system if none is given
power.off: Turn a system off, or every system if none is given (layout-wide stop)
power.get: Print one system's power state as a bare ON/OFF
power.find: Resolve a power system name/prefix/fragment to its full state
power.findr: List every power system whose name matches a regex
power.findg: List every power system whose name matches a shell glob
power.default: Print which power system JMRI treats as the default

roster.list: Show every locomotive in the roster
roster.find: Resolve a locomotive name or DCC address to its roster entry
roster.findr: List every roster entry whose name matches a regex
roster.findg: List every roster entry whose name matches a shell glob

throttle.find: Resolve a locomotive name/fragment/address to its roster identity and cached state
throttle.findr: List every roster locomotive whose name matches a regex
throttle.findg: List every roster locomotive whose name matches a shell glob
throttle.acquire_prefix: Command station prefix (e.g. O, Z, R) to target
throttle.speed: Get or set a locomotive's speed (0-100%%)
throttle.speed_percent: Speed, 0-100 (omit to just read the current speed); a NEGATIVE value is shorthand for reverse at that magnitude, e.g. -40
throttle.stop: Controlled stop of one locomotive, or every touched one if none is given
throttle.stop_loco: Locomotive name, fragment, or DCC address (omit to stop every locomotive this CLI has touched)
throttle.estop: Emergency stop (JMRI decoder e-stop), for one locomotive or every touched one
throttle.estop_loco: Locomotive name, fragment, or DCC address (omit to emergency-stop every locomotive this CLI has touched)
throttle.forward: Set direction forward, for one locomotive or every touched one
throttle.reverse: Set direction reverse, for one locomotive or every touched one
throttle.direction_loco: Locomotive name, fragment, or DCC address (omit to set direction for every locomotive this CLI has touched)
throttle.on: Turn a decoder function on (by number or label), or every labeled one, for one locomotive or every touched one
throttle.off: Turn a decoder function off (by number or label), or every labeled one, for one locomotive or every touched one
throttle.function_loco: Locomotive name, fragment, or DCC address (omit to apply to every locomotive this CLI has touched)
throttle.function: List a locomotive's user-labeled decoder functions
throttle.lights_only: Only switch functions whose label names a light (light/lamp/headlight/lumière/feu/phare) instead of every labeled function
throttle.engine_start: Wake up a locomotive for the session: acquire, face forward, lights on (no speed change), or every touched one if none is given
throttle.engine_start_loco: Locomotive name, fragment, or DCC address (omit to start every locomotive this CLI has touched)
throttle.engine_stop: Ramp down, face forward, lights off, and release one locomotive, or every touched one if none is given
throttle.engine_stop_loco: Locomotive name, fragment, or DCC address (omit to stop every locomotive this CLI has touched)
throttle.sniff: Dump every JMRI WebSocket message live, until Ctrl-C
throttle.sniff_address: DCC address to acquire first (repeatable) so its pushes from OTHER clients show up too; omit to just watch this connection
throttle.sniff_show_pong: Include keepalive pong messages (hidden by default, no info)

light.find: Resolve a light name/fragment/system ID to its full state
light.find_name: Light userName, a fragment of it, or JMRI's system ID (e.g. IL1)
light.findr: List every light whose name matches a regex
light.findg: List every light whose name matches a shell glob
light.on: Turn a light on, or every light if none is given
light.off: Turn a light off, or every light if none is given

turnout.find: Resolve a turnout name or system ID to its full state
turnout.findr: List every turnout whose name matches a regex
turnout.findg: List every turnout whose name matches a shell glob
turnout.close: Close a turnout, or every turnout if none is given
turnout.throw: Throw a turnout, or every turnout if none is given

sensor.find: Resolve a sensor name/fragment/system ID to its full state
sensor.findr: List every sensor whose name matches a regex
sensor.findg: List every sensor whose name matches a shell glob

signal.find: Resolve a signal mast name/fragment/system ID to its full state
signal.findr: List every signal mast whose name matches a regex
signal.findg: List every signal mast whose name matches a shell glob
signal.set: Set a signal mast's aspect (writes to JMRI)
signal.set_aspect: Aspect name, e.g. Hp0/Hp1/Hp2 (not validated locally)

block.find: Resolve a block name/fragment/system ID to its full state
block.findr: List every block whose name matches a regex
block.findg: List every block whose name matches a shell glob

banner.title: jmri-cli v{version} ({url})
banner.line1: Control power, locomotives, lights, turnouts and signals on your JMRI layout.
banner.line2: Also reports roster and sensor info.

cli.shell_welcome: jmri-cli interactive shell. Type `exit`, `quit`, or Ctrl-D to leave.
cli.shell_stop_prompt: {n} loco(s) in motion (address(es) {addrs}). Stop them all before exiting? [Y/n]
cli.shell_exit_no_stop: Exiting without stopping — locomotives left in their current state.
cli.shell_sniff_needs_own_terminal: Error: `throttle sniff` needs its own connection and its own indefinite Ctrl-C loop — run it in a separate terminal instead: `jmri-cli throttle sniff ...`
cli.not_every_system_confirmed: WARNING: not every system confirmed {state_name} after re-read
cli.not_every_entity_confirmed: WARNING: not every {kind} confirmed {state} after re-read
cli.signal_aspect_not_confirmed: WARNING: requested aspect {aspect!r} but observed aspect did not confirm after re-read
cli.turnout_no_feedback_note: Note: command sent OK; some turnouts have no feedback sensor wired up, so JMRI can't confirm their real position (this is expected for those, not a fault) — see the Feedback column.
cli.throttle_no_locos_touched: No locomotives touched yet by this CLI. Run e.g. `jmri-cli throttle speed <loco> <value>` first.
cli.throttle_roster_names_unavailable: Warning: could not fetch roster names; Name column will show "-" for all locomotives.
cli.throttle_no_locos_to_stop: No locomotives touched yet by this CLI, nothing to stop.
cli.throttle_hold_required: Error: --hold is required when setting a nonzero speed outside the interactive shell (use the bare `jmri-cli` shell for an indefinite hold).
cli.throttle_hold_required_address: Error: address={address} is moving — --hold is required to change its direction outside the interactive shell (use the bare `jmri-cli` shell for an indefinite hold).
cli.sniff_listening: Listening for JMRI messages, Ctrl-C to stop...
cli.no_light_labeled_functions: {name} has no light-labeled functions in JMRI's roster (labels are checked for keywords like light/lamp/headlight/lumière/feu/phare) — nothing to switch
cli.session_end_partial_failure: WARNING: session-end had at least one failure — check the output above (some locomotive or system may not have confirmed stopped/off)
cli.cache_already_clean: Nothing to clean — cache files don't exist.
